title: श्री देवी भुजङ्ग स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देवी भुजङ्ग स्तोत्रम् ॥

विरिञ्चाद्रिसंस्थां विनिद्रां विशालां
प्रकाशत्सुवर्णाभलक्ष्मीं त्रिनेत्राम्।
विभूषोज्ज्वलाङ्गीं हिमाद्रेः सुतां तां
पराशक्तिरूपां भजे महिषाघ्नीम्॥ १॥

गलद्रक्तबिन्दूद्भवन्मुण्डमालां
घनश्यामदेहां गिरिस्थां गरिष्ठाम्।
मनोभीतिहन्त्रीं वरामम्बिकां तां
पराशक्तिरूपां भजे महिषाघ्नीम्॥ २॥

ज्वलत्काञ्चनाभां सदानन्दरूपां
समस्तामरीपूजिताङ्घ्रिद्वयार्चाम्।
महादेवपत्नीं महाभैरवाङ्कां
पराशक्तिरूपां भजे महिषाघ्नीम्॥ ३॥

लसच्चन्द्रखण्डोज्ज्वलन्मौलिमालां
त्रिशूलं कृपाणं करैः शङ्खचक्रम्।
धनुर्बाणपाशाङ्कुशौ बिभ्रतीं तां
पराशक्तिरूपां भजे महिषाघ्नीम्॥ ४॥

सुरैः सेवितां शम्भुना चार्चितां तां
मुनीन्द्रस्तुतां योगिनीभिर्वृतां च।
समस्तापदां नाशिनीमम्बिकां तां
पराशक्तिरूपां भजे महिषाघ्नीम्॥ ५॥

शरत्पूर्णचन्द्रप्रभापूर्णवक्त्रां
किरीटोज्ज्वलत्कुण्डलालंकृतास्याम्।
त्रिलोकत्रयीपालनोत्सुकचित्तां
पराशक्तिरूपां भजे महिषाघ्नीम्॥ ६॥

सुसिंहासनस्थां त्रिलोकैकनाथां
समस्तस्य सृष्ट्यादिकर्त्रीमनन्ताम्।
शिवां शान्तरूपां शिवध्यानगम्यां
पराशक्तिरूपां भजे महिषाघ्नीम्॥ ७॥

महादेव्याश्चरणौ प्रपद्ये सदा भक्तिभावेन चित्ते।
स्तवं भुजङ्गप्रयातेन रम्यं पठेद्यः सदा तस्य सर्वार्थसिद्धिः॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचितम् श्री देवी भुजङ्ग स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devi Bhujanga Stotram ||

Virinchadi-samsthaam vinidram vishalam
Prakashat-suvarna-abha-lakshmim trinetram |
Vibhush-ojjvala-angim Himadreih sutam tam
Parashakti-rupam bhaje Mahishaghnim || 1 ||

Galad-rakta-bindu-udbhavan-munda-malam
Ghana-shyama-deham giri-stham garishtham |
Mano-bhiti-hantrim vara-Ambikam tam
Parashakti-rupam bhaje Mahishaghnim || 2 ||

Jvalat-kanchana-abham sada-ananda-rupam
Samasta-amari-pujita-anghri-dvaya-archam |
Mahadeva-patnim Maha-bhairava-ankam
Parashakti-rupam bhaje Mahishaghnim || 3 ||

Lasach-chandra-khanda-ujjvalan-mauli-malam
Trishulam kripaanam karaih shankha-chakram |
Dhanur-bana-pasha-ankushau bibhratim tam
Parashakti-rupam bhaje Mahishaghnim || 4 ||

Suraih sevitam Shambhuna cha-architam tam
Munindra-stutam yoginibhir-vritam cha |
Samasta-apadam nashinim-Ambikam tam
Parashakti-rupam bhaje Mahishaghnim || 5 ||

Sharat-purna-chandra-prabha-purna-vaktram
Kirita-ujjvalat-kundala-alankrita-asyam |
Triloka-trayi-palana-utsuka-chittam
Parashakti-rupam bhaje Mahishaghnim || 6 ||

Su-simhasana-stham tri-loka-eka-natham
Samastasya srishty-adi-kartrim-anantam |
Shivam shanta-rupam Shiva-dhyana-gamyam
Parashakti-rupam bhaje Mahishaghnim || 7 ||

Mahadevyash-charanau prapadye sada bhakti-bhavena chitte |
Stavam bhujanga-prayatena ramyam pathed-yah sada tasya sarvartha-siddhih || 8 ||

|| Iti Shrimach-Shankaracharya-virachitam Shri Devi Bhujanga Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
मेरु पर्वत पर स्थित, जागृत, विशाल, स्वर्णाभ-लक्ष्मी, त्रिनेत्रा, आभूषणों से उज्ज्वल अंगों वाली, हिमालय-पुत्री — उस पराशक्तिरूपा महिषमर्दिनी को भजता हूँ॥ १॥

रक्तबिन्दुओं से उद्भव मुण्डमाला वाली, घनश्याम देह, पर्वत पर स्थित, महान, मनोभय नाशिनी अम्बिका — पराशक्ति को भजता हूँ॥ २॥

ज्वलित कांचन-आभा, सदानन्दरूपा, समस्त देवताओं द्वारा पूजित चरण, महादेव-पत्नी — पराशक्ति महिषाघ्नी को भजता हूँ॥ ३॥

चन्द्रखण्ड से उज्ज्वल मौलिमाला, हाथों में त्रिशूल, कृपाण, शंख, चक्र, धनुष, बाण, पाश, अंकुश धारिणी — पराशक्ति को भजता हूँ॥ ४॥

देवताओं द्वारा सेवित, शम्भु द्वारा अर्चित, मुनीन्द्रों द्वारा स्तुत, योगिनियों से घिरी, समस्त आपदा नाशिनी अम्बिका — पराशक्ति को भजता हूँ॥ ५॥

शरद पूर्णिमा चन्द्र-सा प्रभापूर्ण मुख, किरीट-कुण्डल अलंकृत, त्रिलोक पालन में उत्सुक चित्त — पराशक्ति को भजता हूँ॥ ६॥

सिंहासन पर विराजमान, त्रिलोकनाथा, समस्त सृष्ट्यादि कर्त्री, अनन्ता, शिवा, शान्तरूपा — पराशक्ति को भजता हूँ॥ ७॥

महादेवी के चरणों को भक्तिभाव से प्रणाम करता हूँ। इस भुजंगप्रयात स्तोत्र को जो सदा पढ़ता है उसकी सर्वार्थसिद्धि होती है॥ ८॥
benefits: Invokes Parashakti in her Mahishasuramardini form
Traditionally recited for removal of all fears, obstacles, and calamities
Invokes sarvartha-siddhi — fulfillment of all desires
Composed in melodious Bhujangaprayata metre by Shankaracharya
Invokes the blessing of courage, protection, and divine grace of devi
Ideal for Navaratri and Durga Puja worship
description: The Devi Bhujanga Stotram, known in Sanskrit as श्री देवी भुजङ्ग स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Goddess Durga. Drawn from Devi Bhujanga Stotram — Adi Shankaracharya, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to invoke Parashakti in her Mahishasuramardini form and grant sarvartha-siddhi — fulfillment of all desires. It is also said to bestow courage, protection, and divine grace of Devi. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri, Durga Puja, and Vijayadashami. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 6 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Bhujanga Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Devi Bhujanga Stotram by Adi Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Serpentine hymn to Goddess Durga Mahishasuramardini.
relatedSearches: Bhujangam meter snake crawl Sanskrit poetry

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Devi Bhujanga Stotram named after a snake?
seoFaqs.answer: The title refers not to a serpent deity but to the Sanskrit meter in which the hymn is composed, called Bhujanga Prayata, literally 'the gliding of a snake'. As Hindupedia and Sanskrit Documents explain, the meter has a fixed cadence of four ya-ganas per line (three syllables of one short and two long), producing a continuous rolling rhythm that imitates a snake's smooth motion. Adi Shankaracharya was a recognised master of this meter and used it for several of his most musical hymns. The Devi Bhujanga Stotram applies that meter to praise of the Devi enthroned on the Sri Chakra, so the name encodes both the form (snake-meter) and the form's effect on recitation: an unbroken, gliding chant suited to deep meditative absorption.

seoFaqs.question: Which form of the Devi does the Bhujanga Stotram address?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is addressed to the supreme Devi as enthroned in the Sri Chakra, generally identified with Lalita Tripura Sundari in Sri Vidya parampara. As Hindupedia notes explicitly, this is 'a prayer addressed to the Goddess of Sri Chakra'. Shankara progressively visualises her seated on the bindu of the yantra, surrounded by the avarana devatas, with classical Sri Vidya iconography (sugarcane bow, flowery arrows, noose and goad). The stotram therefore reads as a Bhujanga-meter rendering of Sri Vidya dhyana, complementing his other Sri Vidya works such as Saundarya Lahari and Tripura Sundari Ashtakam. Shankara's authorship places it within his recognised Devi-stotra corpus catalogued on Sanskrit Documents.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Devi Bhujanga Stotram differ from Saundarya Lahari?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are Shankara compositions praising the Devi of Sri Chakra, but they differ in scale, meter and function. Saundarya Lahari is a 100-verse magnum opus in shikharini and other meters that systematically maps the Sri Chakra and includes Tantric yantras and prayoga at each verse, traditionally treated as a sadhana text. Devi Bhujanga Stotram is shorter, written entirely in Bhujanga Prayata, and is purely contemplative-devotional rather than prayoga-laden. Devotees often chant the Bhujanga Stotram daily as a melodic praise of the same Devi without the technical Tantric framework required by Saundarya Lahari, which makes it accessible to non-initiates. Sanskrit Documents and Stotram.co.in list both works under Shankara's Devi corpus.

seoFaqs.question: Why did Adi Shankara favour the Bhujanga meter for several stotras?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhujanga Prayata is unusually well suited to recitation because its rolling, equal-paced cadence carries the chanter's voice in long unbroken phrases, producing what Sanskrit poetics calls a prasada-guna or 'easy-flowing' quality. Shankara used this meter for at least four major hymns including Subramanya Bhujangam, Shiva Bhujangam, Devi Bhujangam and Vishnu Bhujangam, as Sanskrit Documents catalogues. The choice was deliberate: a meter that imitates a snake's smooth motion sustains the meditative breath, helps memorisation, and produces a hypnotic effect when chanted aloud, which suits hymns intended for daily nitya parayana. The Bhujanga set therefore functions as Shankara's standard-format devotional cycle across the major deities.

seoFaqs.question: Is the Devi Bhujanga Stotram suitable for daily recitation by householders?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. Because the stotram is praise-form rather than mantric prayoga and does not invoke Tantric bija syllables outside Sri Vidya initiation, it is regarded as safe for daily recitation by any sincere devotee. Stotram.co.in and Hindupedia both publish it openly with translation, and Shaktas commonly chant it after morning bath as part of their Devi parayana, often paired with Lalita Sahasranama or Khadgamala. The melodic Bhujanga meter rewards aloud recitation, so practitioners are encouraged to chant rather than read silently. As with all Devi stotras, the standard recommendation is to face east or north, light a lamp, and close with the Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotram to compensate for any errors in pronunciation or attention.

seoFaqs.question: Where can one find an authentic Sanskrit text of the Devi Bhujanga Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Reliable Sanskrit editions are hosted on Sanskritdocuments.org under the shankarAchArya collection, on Hindupedia's Devi Bhujanga Stotram entry, and on Stotram.co.in with Devanagari and IAST transliteration. For English translation with verse-by-verse commentary, P. R. Ramachander's Stotraratna (Raja Thatha) blog provides a widely cited English rendering. Print editions are published by Chinmaya Publications and Ramakrishna Math as part of their Shankara Stotra Manjari volumes. Devotees seeking a definitive text are advised to cross-check at least one digital source against a print edition, since the Bhujanga meter occasionally invites scribal variants when copyists try to fit the strict three-syllable-foot pattern across lines.
